metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 6 H
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Breeder, employer, applicant, titleholder, licensee, and commercialization-agent roles remain distinct; rights are not reassigned to a later employer, and no external communication was made.

profiles.ownershipType: public-institution fruit breeder with rights split by program
profiles.ownershipSummary: Moriyuki Shoda worked on NARO pear releases and later Okinawa Prefecture pineapple breeding. NARO retains the cited pear-program rights, while Okinawa Prefecture is the named titleholder for OKINO P19; neither portfolio should be transferred to Shoda or to his later employer.
profiles.headquarters: Nago, Okinawa, Japan (current public research branch); legacy pear program at NARO, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: Current pineapple work is conducted in a multi-breeder Okinawa Prefecture team, and the older pear work sits in NARO's institutional program; both provide institutional bench rather than a personal succession opportunity.
profiles.primaryContactName: Okinawa Prefectural Agricultural Research Center, Nago Branch
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-program profile. The 2021 MAFF record names Okinawa Prefecture as titleholder and Shoda as one of nine breeders. Treat Narumi and other NARO pear work as legacy NARO IP, not Okinawa property. Cultivar names are denominations; no supported Shoda-owned trademark was found.

profiles.ownershipType: private peach farmer-breeder and nursery operator
profiles.ownershipSummary: The name is strongly matched to Lee Yoon-do/Lee Yundo, chief executive of Gyeongbok Seedling Farm and developer of 16 peach varieties. Public reporting establishes the operating affiliation but does not establish cultivar-level legal title, so the farm is a candidate parent rather than a confirmed rightsholder.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Owner-led breeding profile with clear key-person concentration; no public successor or broader breeding bench was identified. His honorary public-research role does not move his private breeding rights to the institute.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Public award coverage identifies the Lee Yoon-do romanization variant as the farm's chief executive and a peach breeder, but no registry title record or assignment was found for the cited cultivars.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Likely romanization variants are Lee Youn Do, Lee Yoon-do, and Lee Yundo. Verify Korean PVP title, farm legal form, address, direct business contact, and any family or employee succession before treating the portfolio as owned by the farm. Cultivar denominations are not presented as trademarks.

profiles.ownershipType: Israeli government research engineer and grape breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Oded Degani is a Fruit Tree Sciences research engineer at the Volcani Center/Agricultural Research Organization. The cited Black Glory and Green Onyx patents were assigned by the breeder team to the State of Israel's Agricultural Research Organization, not held personally.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Member of an active multi-researcher table-grape breeding department with institutional continuity; no personal ownership or standalone succession signal was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institutional profile. Patent assignment evidence supports ARO/State of Israel ownership for the cited protected cultivars; confirm each recent VARO-series PBR separately. Cultivar denominations are not presented as Degani-owned marks.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Tatiana Okun is an ARO/Volcani table-grape breeder. Exact-name patent records show her assigning Black Glory and Green Onyx with the breeder team to the State of Israel's Agricultural Research Organization; a current department roster appears to use the spelling Tatiana Ockon.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Researcher within an active public Fruit Tree Sciences breeding team, with institutional bench and no personal title or standalone succession signal.
profiles.primaryContactName: Agricultural Research Organization, Fruit Tree Sciences
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat Okun and Ockon as a supported spelling-variant match because the exact Okun name appears on the patents and Ockon appears in the same current department, but retain the alias caveat. No public direct business contact was located.

profiles.ownershipType: grower-breeder and member of a strategic-scale grower cooperative
profiles.ownershipSummary: Gregory Miller is an Ohio grower-breeder associated with Empire Chestnut Company and the Midwest Apple Improvement Association. MAIA, not Miller personally, is the documented assignee for MAIA1 and the cooperative's protected apple portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Carrollton, Ohio, United States (Miller business); Ohio, United States (MAIA program)
profiles.leadershipSummary: MAIA has elected leadership, hundreds of grower members, and a broad breeding and evaluation network, so the program is institutionally durable rather than dependent on Miller alone.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Name order is Gregory Miller. Roll protected MAIA genetics into MAIA, while preserving Empire Chestnut Company as Miller's current operating affiliation. The official MAIA site supports the commercial variety names; this review did not determine mark ownership beyond the association's use.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The initials resolve to Assoc. Prof. Zdravko Nakov of the Institute of Viticulture and Enology - Pleven, Agricultural Academy. The sources support employer and breeding-program affiliation but do not establish cultivar-level legal title, so no rights are assigned to Nakov personally or conclusively to the institute without the Bulgarian register.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Active public-institute breeder working with a multi-person vine-selection team; institutional bench is visible and no personal succession opportunity is indicated.
profiles.primaryContactName: Institute of Viticulture and Enology - Pleven
profiles.candidateParent: Institute of Viticulture and Enology - Pleven, Agricultural Academy
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Current and historical publications identify Zdravko Nakov with the institute's vine-selection program, but the reviewed sources do not provide a holder or assignment field for his cited cultivars.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public-institution profile. Verify each Bulgarian or CPVO record for applicant, holder, status, and any state succession before rollup. The listed names are cultivar or clone denominations, not supported trademarks.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Paolo Sbrighi is a technical collaborator in CREA's Forli strawberry breeding program. CREA is a documented titleholder, sometimes jointly with universities, regional agencies, or producer partners; Sbrighi is not shown as a personal owner.
profiles.headquarters: Forli, Emilia-Romagna, Italy (work site); CREA headquarters in Rome, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Forli program is a multi-person team led publicly by Gianluca Baruzzi, with technical and scientific bench beyond Sbrighi.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institutional profile. Determine titleholder and partner shares cultivar by cultivar; do not collapse joint CREA, university, regional-agency, or cooperative rights into Sbrighi. Cultivar denominations are not presented as his trademarks.

profiles.ownershipType: public botanical-garden kiwifruit breeder and former institutional leader
profiles.ownershipSummary: Junjie Gong is historically documented at Wuhan Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences, including as a deputy director. Wuhan Botanical Garden/CAS is the cited titleholder for Jinyan and Dong Hong and has granted commercialization licenses for related kiwifruit; those licenses do not make Gong or a later licensee the owner.
profiles.headquarters: Wuhan Botanical Garden, No. 201 Jiufeng 1 Road, East Lake High-Tech Development Zone, Wuhan, Hubei, China
profiles.leadershipSummary: Historical senior leader within a public institutional breeding program; the current kiwifruit group has named researchers and a shared program contact, evidencing continuing bench after Gong's active breeding period.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institutional profile. Title evidence is cultivar-specific but directly supports WBG/CAS for Jinyan and Dong Hong. Keep historic worldwide propagation/commercialization licensees separate from ownership. No current personal role or personal title was found for Gong.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Shengmei Wang is historically documented in Wuhan Botanical Garden/CAS kiwifruit breeding. Wuhan Botanical Garden/CAS, not Wang personally, is the cited titleholder for Jinyan and Dong Hong and the institution that licensed commercialization of related cultivars.
profiles.headquarters: Wuhan Botanical Garden, No. 201 Jiufeng 1 Road, East Lake High-Tech Development Zone, Wuhan, Hubei, China
profiles.leadershipSummary: Legacy researcher in a continuing public kiwifruit program with a current multi-researcher bench; no personal successor or current personal role was located.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institutional profile. Preserve WBG/CAS as holder where the PBR record says so and keep licensees separate. Cultivar names and the Oriental Red commercial synonym are not presented as Wang-owned marks.

profiles.ownershipType: independent individual apple breeder and rightsholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Takao Takano is the Iwate breeder of Takano 6 and related numbered apples. The Takano 6 U.S. patent identifies Takano as inventor, records the assignee as an individual, and cites Japanese PBR 27547; no employer or corporate owner was found.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The public record presents an individual breeder with material key-person concentration; no company, team bench, or named successor was identified.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Independent-rightsholder profile. Confirm the exact Japanese holder line, live status, agents, licenses, and direct public business contact for each numbered cultivar. The cultivar denominations are not treated as verified trademarks.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Carlo Bergamini is a CREA viticulture researcher at Turi. CREA's plant-IP catalog names CREA as titleholder for Triviani and lists Bergamini in the breeder team; commercialization partners such as NuVaUT are not thereby rightsholders.
profiles.headquarters: Turi, Bari, Italy (work site); CREA headquarters in Rome, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: Active member of a multi-scientist CREA table-grape breeding team with institutional continuity and no personal ownership or standalone succession signal.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institutional profile. Verify title and any joint applicant, foreign agent, or license by cultivar. The catalog supports CREA title for Triviani; do not roll CREA rights into Bergamini or a commercialization partner.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact name could not be responsibly linked to a unique plant breeder, entity, employer, or rightsholder from the available public-web and local source context.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not assign an employer, candidate parent, titleholder, contact, portfolio, or succession signal. A crop, cultivar denomination, application or grant number, jurisdiction, Korean-script name, or romanization variant is required to disambiguate.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The initials resolve to Zdravko D. Zankov, a historical Bulgarian grape breeder working with state viticulture researchers. Public ampelographic sources support institutional breeding and state approval, but not personal title or a complete modern succession chain for the old cultivars.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Historical team breeder working with Kunyu Stoev and other public researchers; present institutional successor and live-rights bench are cultivar dependent and were not established.
profiles.candidateParent: Bulgarian public viticulture breeding institutions, cultivar dependent
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Historical official-program sources place Zdravko Zankov in the Department of Viticulture at Septemvri and describe state approval or release, but they do not provide modern holder or assignment fields.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Legacy public-program profile. Many records are old, surrendered, or likely term-expired; verify current Bulgarian register status and legal successor per cultivar. Do not treat Zankov as personal owner or infer that today's Pleven institute automatically inherited every historical right.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The initial and surname are insufficient to distinguish a unique breeder or establish an employer or rightsholder. A bibliographic match to a J. Tsvetkov in Bulgarian viticulture is not enough to assign this profile.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not assign the Septemvri bibliographic match, an employer, candidate parent, titleholder, contact, portfolio, or succession signal without crop, cultivar, application number, jurisdiction, or full given name.

profiles.ownershipType: independent individual stone-fruit breeder and sole proprietor
profiles.ownershipSummary: Angelo Minguzzi is a private Romagna breeder and is personally named as titleholder for Pit Lane, Pit Stop, Puntocom, and Puntoit in protected-variety records. Geoplant's exclusive propagation and commercialization license for Albinova is a license, not a transfer of Minguzzi's underlying breeder identity or all portfolio rights.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Owner-led, small-scale profile with visible key-person concentration and no confirmed successor. Historical applications naming Giulio Minguzzi may indicate family participation, but do not establish succession or current title without an assignment chain.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Independent-rightsholder profile. Verify the live title and assignment chain for each cultivar, especially records where the original Italian applicant was Giulio Minguzzi but a later CPVO holder list names Angelo. Confirm sole-proprietor continuity, direct business contact, license scope, and whether any commercial name is separately trademarked.
